title: Anny D. Rodriguez, Ph.D., MPH
text:
Strengths I Bring
Beyond technical tools, I bring a strong ability to connect analytic work with meaningful public health questions. I enjoy translating complex data into clear insights, collaborating across disciplines, and designing work that is rigorous, reproducible, and useful in practice.

title: Anny D. Rodriguez, Ph.D., MPH
text:
I am always looking for ways to collaborate, please feel free to reach me using any of the following:

Currently at: 🏛 Morehouse School of Medicine

title: What I Do
section: AI and Machine Learning for Cancer Screening Promotion
text:
AI and Machine Learning for Cancer Screening Promotion
I have developed analytic approaches to better understand factors associated with breast and cervical cancer screening behaviors. This work integrates demographic, clinical, and behavioral data to identify patterns that can inform more targeted and equitable intervention strategies.
Focus areas:

Predictive modeling
Risk factor identification
Screening behavior analysis
Health disparities

title: What I Do
text:
Clinical Trial Recruitment Optimization
My research has examined recruitment conversations and participation barriers among underrepresented populations in clinical research. Using computational methods and applied analytics, I explore how communication patterns, trust, and structural barriers shape participation in research.
Focus areas:

Representation in clinical research
Recruitment analytics
Natural language processing applications
Health equity

title: What I Do
section: Population Health Analytics Using National Survey Data
text:
Population Health Analytics Using National Survey Data
I use large-scale public health datasets, including survey data, to investigate determinants of preventive care, developmental outcomes, and access-related barriers. These projects emphasize rigorous statistical methods and public health relevance.
Focus areas:

Survey-weighted analysis
Preventive care utilization
Child and maternal health
Population-level disparities

title: What I Do
section: Public Health Informatics and Data Science Education
text:
Public Health Informatics and Data Science Education
In addition to applied research, I build practical training experiences that prepare students to use modern analytic tools in public health settings. This includes curriculum development in R, reproducible reporting, and health data analysis.
Focus areas:

Reproducible workflows
Applied R programming
Data communication
Workforce development


Project Approach
Across projects, I aim to connect rigorous analytic methods with real-world impact. My work is grounded in strong epidemiologic thinking, modern data science methods, and a commitment to improving population health through actionable insight.

title: Education
text:
Education

Ph.D., Public Health (Epidemiology)
Florida International University

C.V. Starr Scholarship Reciepient - 2020-2024

Master of Public Health (MPH), Epidemiology
Florida International University
Bachelor of Science in Public Health
University of South Florida




Experience

Assistant Professor — Morehouse School of Medicine
Department of Public Health Education · Atlanta, GA · July 2024 – Present

Lead applied research and graduate education at the intersection of epidemiology, data science, and health equity.
Teach graduate courses in Epidemiology, Biostatistics, Research Methods, and Applied R Programming (MPH 716), and develop a new Advanced Data Visualization course.
Design and lead the Epidemiology and Health Data Science concentration curriculum.
Conduct observational studies using BRFSS, NHANES, and clinical data to evaluate cancer screening disparities and health outcomes.
Build reproducible analytic pipelines in R, Quarto, and GitHub; mentor MPH students in data analysis and manuscript development.
Recipient of the AUC Data Science Initiative Summer Curriculum Development Award (2024).

Key areas: R · Quarto · GitHub · BRFSS/NHANES · Regression modeling · Curriculum design · Health disparities


AHA Diversity in Clinical Trials Postdoctoral Fellow
Stanford University & Morehouse School of Medicine · Atlanta, GA · Jan 2024 – Jul 2024

Applied machine learning and large language models (LLMs) to analyze health datasets in collaboration with cardiologists and cancer epidemiologists.
Co-authored publications on LLM evaluation for cancer communication and ML-based recruitment analysis, including work published in JMIR Cancer and JMIR Formative Research.
Contributed to the TOTAL clinical trials diversity protocol, now published in Contemporary Clinical Trials (2026).
Mentored junior researchers in data science methods and contributed to grant writing and conference presentations.

Key areas: Python · NLP/LLMs · Machine learning · Clinical trials · Health equity


National Teaching Fellow in Data Science
NIMHD/RCMI-funded · Morehouse School of Medicine & Florida International University · Sep 2022 – Present

Funded by the National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities (NIMHD) through the RCMI network to advance data science education in minority-serving institutions.
Designed and taught a national R programming curriculum.
Led Software Carpentry workshops at the University of Miami and a 10-week national RCMI course.
Presented on R for data science in public health at the International Data Science Conference Europe 2022 in Belgrade, Serbia.

Key areas: R programming education · NIMHD · RCMI · Workforce development


COVID-19 Data Analyst — Florida International University
Department of Epidemiology · Miami, FL · Aug 2021 – Dec 2023

Analyzed large-scale surveillance data from the Florida Department of Health and CDC across Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties.
Built an interactive R Shiny dashboard to visualize real-time COVID-19 trends for municipal and county officials; the dashboard was published as a citable output (Rodriguez et al., 2022).
Developed reproducible data workflows for continuous monitoring and reporting.
Contributed to the FIU 3MT Top 10 Finalist presentation (2023).

Key areas: R/Shiny · CDC/FLDOH data · Surveillance · Dashboard design


Clinical Research Coordinator — University of Miami, Miller School of Medicine
Department of Neurology · Miami, FL · Jun 2019 – Aug 2020

Coordinated NIH- and industry-sponsored stroke clinical trials involving more than 100 participants, including recruitment, screening, and follow-up.
Worked with EHR data (Epic, Cerner) to support patient-level outcome analyses.
Oversaw IRB submissions, regulatory compliance, and protocol documentation.

Key areas: EHR (Epic/Cerner) · REDCap · Clinical trials · IRB/Compliance


Research Support Specialist — University of Miami, Miller School of Medicine
Department of Neurology · Miami, FL · Aug 2018 – Jun 2019

Conducted statistical analyses using SAS and SPSS on stroke and registry datasets.
Designed and managed REDCap databases for clinical data collection workflows.
Coordinated multi-site data sharing with hospitals statewide.
Organized the Florida Stroke Registry Annual Meeting.

Key areas: SAS · SPSS · REDCap · Multi-site coordination


Research Analyst — Bascom Palmer Eye Institute
Department of Biostatistics · Miami, FL · Aug 2016 – Aug 2018

Performed statistical analyses for more than 20 NIH- and NEI-funded ophthalmic studies using SPSS and REDCap.
Co-authored peer-reviewed publications, including work published in the Journal of Alzheimer’s Disease (2018).
Maintained IRB compliance documentation and contributed to conference abstracts and manuscripts.
